speaker: MATT
raw_text: If you move quickly, you can probably get there right around sunset.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A little sticky.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A little salsa?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: A little burny?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How many fingers am I holding up?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I think four?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How many members of The Mighty Nein are there?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But two are always gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I feel like when I see someone else throw up I feel almost like I do (retching). No?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's a violent reaction.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Jester, help her out there, she's in need.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want me to stick something down your throat?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No, I mean, I don't know if that will help. I think it was just gross to see (retching). Uh-oh. It's still happening.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is it just seeing someone throw up or is it also hearing the sound that makes it worse? If I just went (retching) does that--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I think it's the sound, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Looking at our map, how much longer do I think we have to go to get to our destination?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: There are clouds in the sky, especially at this time of year, it's generally pretty cloudy here in the Empire. You've seen breaks in the clouds as you've traveled for most of the day, it's been warmer than usual, so it's been a welcome change of pace from what you're used to, but this is not characteristic of cloud cover, as most everything here has some level of shade.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll take a watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll sit down, I've been standing for such a long time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's true, give those ankles a rest. Who's helming the roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll helm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're aiding Beau. Roll with advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 17, all right. Continuing your pace, Beau as you're watching out, from a north-northwestern direction, you see a dark shape come over one of the hilltops, wide and encompassing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Moving?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It seems to be. A number of miles out of the way, almost on the horizon, you can see this series of rolling hills in that direction that obscure what lies beyond that area. You watch as one of the hills goes dark on top, like the coloration gets covered in shade.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What does the weather look like? Are there clouds in the sky?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You both look out there and as you travel for the next ten minutes or so, you can see faint glimmers in that moving shadow as a bit of sunlight travels over it. It's at that moment you realize what this is. It's a legion. What was a dark shadow appears to be hundreds and hundreds of soldiers, an army regiment that is shifting from the northwestern side of the Empire to cross over the byway, heading east. As you continue your pace forward, you get to a point where you may intersect with their path if you continue, or you can slow down or speed up. It's your choice.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Still rolling grasslands, or are there trees off to the side?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are bits of trees and clusters of them, nothing that would be considered a major forest. What you slept in the night before has probably been the largest cluster you've seen in your travel today so far. This catches you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It looks like the landscape is going dark? Not something moved in front of it, or like something is moving?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct. Best you can tell at this distance.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Who's got good eyes and knows about clouds and shit?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb knows about clouds.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I've spent a long time on the road, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does that look natural to you?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you hit that portion of the road, you're having a hard time steering the horses, you're not entirely familiar enough to pull on the rein of multiple at a single time to get the shift and movement. They begin to careen a little bit off of the path, almost trying to hide into the grass embankment which slows your movement down ever so slightly, before (impact) one of the wheels suddenly (impact and scraping). It is now digging into the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's a very good point.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: If we move, though, we have to move fast, and maybe a little bit carefully.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go! Go!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With that (galloping) the horses begin to pick up pace. You all grab the sides of the cart as it begins to whir and kick its way across the street. The horses galloping, framing the two different sides of the cart as you pick up speed. You come up over one of the hills and then down another embankment. As you curve around the side, there's a part where the grass has overgrown a large majority of the road here, but you coast through the center.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should stand up and ride it like a surfboard!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's not do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Like Teen Wolf?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a general animal handling check if you don't mind, Molly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Seven?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's terrible.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You probably have about ten minutes before they cross over the byway.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Guys, I'm amazing!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you are!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That would have been way more amazing if I'd remembered that I could do this. Let me try it on this other wheel, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, we have two broken wheels?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, one was partially damaged, the other one was pretty wrecked. The other wheel, unfortunately, has taken a fair amount of damage from the impact, and the lower half of it has broken inward, it's splintered out and it's bent its way off of the axle. Unfortunately, it's a bit too big for the Mending cantrip to fix.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I try to punch it into place?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's pretty wrecked. You do have three wheels, which means you could try it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: One of us could be a wheel?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yasha curls up around the axle.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How far away is the army?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No, you've been traveling for half a day since you left, so you've pretty much sobered up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I agree, what's our story?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm escorting you to the Labenda Swamp. Right? To retrieve stuff for the crown? For Zadash?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. We needed help because our family is in the swamp.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: We could say we were helping out-- what was her name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're just traveling hired hands. We are going to go to the swamp and look for yard work.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You should move the safe.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: In the swamp?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I push the safe off the cart.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Yasha and Jester should take the safe.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (horse galloping)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are there any woods or anywhere to hide?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's high grass. The grass off the road where you guys are standing right now is, for you, almost shoulder-high.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to go in the grass.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Stealth check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm just going to sit on the wagon looking bored.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I just bought it. It's in the bag.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: 22. Am I still drunk? Do I need to--?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: They turn around and they make their way back up to the rest of the army. About 25 minutes or so it takes for the group of soldiers to finally cross over the path.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course. Come on." They get back on their horses and they ride back. At this point, the regiment has now crossed over the road, and you can now see hundreds of soldiers going across the Byway. The captain gives a nod, puts his helmet back on. "Well, I wish you all luck. Stay safe and I hope that if luck is kind to us, we'll cross paths again."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Stay safe as well. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Stay safe.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Bye.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Everybody be safe.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: And it does. Those who are taking the first part of your evening's rest come to as the storm begins to come over. The weather gets colder, you feel the rain imminent within the next hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So that we don't get wet?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Full tauntaun style.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are there any caves nearby? I mean, we're kind of by a thing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're in the middle of a field right now. It's hilly terrain and the topography shifts a bit, but right now all you see is darkness beyond your immediate darkvision peripheral.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're going to get wet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How many of us can fit under that cart, I wonder.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Even then, the ground's going to get--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Disgusting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Soaking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The occasional flash of light you see in the distance begins to frame an ominous shape far to the north: the beginnings of a mountain range that you have been able to see previously or hadn't paid attention with the darkening sky.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did you guys wake us up because of the storm, I'm assuming?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think the storm's going to wake you up soon enough.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, the cloak you have is treated with simple wax to try and resist most water. You'll probably be a bit soggy in the morning, but it's doable.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're going here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: These are not explosions, orange or red?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, these were lightning bolts in the distance in the storm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh there's Druvenlode! There's Max Load.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Silberquel Ridge, perhaps? Hupperdook is beyond that mountain range.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does that mean that guy was named after a whole city?! Does that means he owns a city?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He was from Druvenlode.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I thought that was his last name.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: His name was Captain Maximilian.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, if he was taking the Leonardo da Vinci approach then it could be accurate. Max of Lode!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hmm. I think we just need to hunker down.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll take the next watch because I don't mind the rain.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The rain begins to kick in your vicinity.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll take next watch, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The fire begins to go out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm getting underneath the cart.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me too!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to get on the cart and try and put my cloak over me.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point it's midnight, so it's pitch black.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm staying up. I like the rain.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I look at Molly underneath me on the cart and I fart a little bit. Just a little.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm down here, too! (farting noise)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Not a little one. Don't gamble on those.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Alcohol and beef jerky, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: At least I'll definitely notice the smell like fucking roses.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The first watch goes beyond the rain without incident. Who is taking the second watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It is a big group of us, yeah? Is there enough people left over to do the last watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll take last.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, there's only been the first watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Which was Molly and I, next one is Caleb and Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I didn't hear that part. So Caleb and Jester, who's keeping up? Who's the point man on that watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, your eyesight is better than mine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make your check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Just regular plain old check?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You wake up. You're shivering a bit from the cold and the night air. The ground has dampened, even beneath the cart. You were sleeping beneath the cart, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay, okay, okay. Holy moly.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you come to consciousness through this evening's rest for your final watch, as you gather after that sleep is probably not going to find its way to you again for the rest of the evening. Who is taking the final watch with you?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay, okay, okay. Holy moly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You wake up. You're shivering a bit from the cold and the night air. The ground has dampened, even beneath the cart. You were sleeping beneath the cart, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Near it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did she go back to New York?!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm here!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay, good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That would be fucked up, if you used that as your exit. Wait, where-- what do you mean Ashley left?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You forgot to say goodbye. She's just gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Never say goodbye!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So was she a Xhorhasian spy and are we all dead in our sleep?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll a perception check-- no.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it water or blood that we're covered in?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, you can get there. A little jaunt down there. Soaking wet as the rain still pours down. You approach and you come to the half-splintered form of one of the larger trees in this section. There are a few dotted singular trees or clusters around, but this was once a tall and proud tree. As you approach, you can see near the top it's been carved right down the center and part of it has splintered and exploded. There are chunks of wood around the vicinity and you can see even amongst the rain, on the interior of the exposed trunk there are still faintly orange glowing embers from where the bolt had struck through the night.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How you holding up? Can I go under you? Can you be like a shelter for me? Like a lean-to?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Sure, if you want to get under my hood here?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wing?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Well, it doesn't come out all the time.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know how it works.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's just if I get really mad.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But your arm is probably big enough to shield me from the rain. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Are you dry?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, yes. I don't like to get wet.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I feel like I don't know if I want to keep doing this.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Could you give me a moment?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Give you a moment?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to go to the tree over there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you have to take a piss?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, I won't look.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I look.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I go where near the tree was struck close to us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's about a quarter-mile walk from where you are, but you can head there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's not that long of a walk, quarter mile.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You can take it and carve in the edge there using the tip. It takes you a few minutes to do so, but you manage to complete the shell of the symbol's outline and then make your trek back to join up with Nott.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (whispered) And a baseball bat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (whispered) Lemonade.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I heard you, and I wanted to thank you for saving me, and I promise you I will be the best servant you ever had. Thank you. I'm going to take a piece of the bark and I'd like to carve the symbol into the tree, the Stormlord symbol. Not very good, but just to have it there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The bark is a harder utensil to utilize to carve into bark.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, I take a piece of the bark to have and I use my sword.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As Nott cuddles up alongside Yasha's comparably hulking form to keep out of the water, you finish your final watch as you both watch the sun begin to rise over the horizon-- or at least, you can't see the sun, but you can see the day grows lighter as it makes its way over the cloud cover. The rain is not quite as strong, and the lightning seems to have subsided, but it is still a light drizzle when it comes to the proper morning. As the rest of you come to consciousness, completing your long rest, the day is yours, damp as it may be. The low fog and mist still encompasses most of the valley around you, and it's a beautiful sight as you glance around first thing in the morning. You can see only the tops of the hills slowly breaking through the layer of cold mist that hits the lower valleys between that shifting topography. As you look off, you can now have a much better view of the Silberquel Ridge above this large mountain range that marks the geological center of the empire. As you glance down, you can barely make out what might be the swamp.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, just the bottom bits.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, they come in many different shapes and sizes. The cobblers throw them away when they're bad. You can fish them out of the trash and you can have a whole bunch of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What do you use them for?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Nothing. I mean, you can play a little drum thing with them.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: And should you need to resole your shoes--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure, if you string them together, they make a Roman gladiator belt sort of a thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Have you ever put them one on top of the other and make a platform shoe?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, I could be a little bit taller! I think if you put two on top of each other and push them in, it looks like lips are talking!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Cool! Well.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I feel like we've gotten to know each other so well!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Very much so. Do you want to get back under here?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, under your gigantic arm, thank you.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About half an hour or so of travel along the road into the center of Labenda Swamp, the humid air prevents the mounting sweat on your body from evaporating, causing your sweat to pool in your armor in uncomfortable ways. Your breathing is somewhat shallow as you adapt to the moisture in the air around you. The ever-present hum of the buzzing insects begins to permeate the atmosphere around you, while the musky stench of decaying plants and stagnant water grow stronger the further in you pass. A very low fog, since it's still relatively early in the day, sits across the surface of the water that's exposed in patches between where the thick roots and soft rotting vegetation seems to be sitting, like a spongy ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I thought there wouldn't be insects because it's cold!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: In addition to the insects, do we start to become aware of wildlife around? Birds and beasts?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do we know anything about where we're going?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're going to see a safe house that the lights have been out at.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're trying to find Febron Keyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but it's a big swamp. Are any of us good at like wood stuff? Is this me? Am I the girl?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm good at nature. I've lived in the woods for a long time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm okay at surviving.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Ten? That's enough. Over the edge you glance down and you can see past the tree line, maybe about 100 or so feet from where you are, there's a small clearing, this open area. Where the water is, there is a thick bit of black bog. It looks almost like a thick swampy, tar-ish little pool. You can see a part of a tree has fallen into it, and the liquid is generally darkening the bottom of where it's been stuck. You don't know how long it's been there. There is the dark root, and crooked branches, and elements of it sticking up in the air. The smell of bog rot is very strong, even from this proximity to it. You see something moving in the center of this bog, struggling, shifting. You see, covered in this tar-like material and flailing, some sort of humanoid creature of jet black skin. It's not skin-- feathers. It's fighting against the swamp, it's small. Shifting out of the water off to the side, slowly up to the bog, probably what Beau saw earlier, some sort of reptilian creature with a long snout and a long tail. Curving around and making its way slowly towards the bog. As the flailing bird creature is attempting to give a (panicked bird croaking).

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But the safe house is in the swamp, it's not in Berleben, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As far as you know, yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We press on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Maybe not calling out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we can climb a tree! Are there any tall trees?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are a number of tall trees.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I start climbing a tree?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure, you want to get a little closer and climb a tree?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a general athletics check as you jump off the side, your legs (slurping) into the swamp, as they sink about a foot with each step, leaving these heavy footprints behind that fill with water.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You make your way through the swamp, the suction doesn't stop you one bit. You grab the nearby tree and climb to the top and get off to a branch and look in the direction. Make a perception check, please. That's cocked. How could you do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It was a really good one if it hadn't been. Perception ten.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That ends your turn. It is now the crocs' turns. (counts movement) It actually climbs up the rock after you, (stomping) and then goes and takes a swipe down at you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: First strike--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: --is 17 plus four, 21. Plus I'll use Fury of the Small, making it 26.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How often do you get to add that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Just once a day. Then second does not get sneak attack? Even though it's at disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sneak attack, you only get once per turn. Not per round, per turn. So you can still get it on a reaction.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Six, but that's doubled?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Whatever you rolled on the dice, doubled.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Four, so eight. Eight for that one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Eight points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Then one more. That's ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ten points of damage. This poor gator turned around, went blind, got shot multiple times, blasted with fiery beams.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It still can't stop thinking about Mollymauk, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's just so focused on him. That ends your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to move behind that rock over there. (yelping) That's it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That is a 16 on the saving throw for the blind crocodile, so it does resist the blindness. Its vision comes back. However, it fucked it up for a round. Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nice. As you release the two bolts, they manage to peek around the tree, see it grasping Nott in its jaws and swinging her back and forth. One hits the bottom of the jaw, and the other hits its side. You see this little splatter of dark crimson alligator blood from the two impacts, but it's still holding taut to this tiny little morsel it has in its jaws.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll use my pathetic speed to try and match Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Want to move Caleb up for me, Beau? Somebody?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Caleb, or Fjord?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry. Meant to say Fjord to Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, this is so much fun. I'm glad we are helping this random-ass penguin.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At least you guys have range. That brings us to the top again. Molly and Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You want to go first?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I get to this guy who's got Nott?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'm going to parkour off.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It looks at it. (bird croaking) It begins to try and pull its way back out of the bog. It's getting stuck and sliding in, but it's trying to pull itself backward. That's your turn, Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure. That is a natural 19.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no. (Infernal coughing)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A swamp bug goes into your throat as you're trying speak it, and you have to cough it out for a second. That finishes Molly's run. Jester, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'm going to Invoke Duplicity as my action, and I'm going to pop my little person down right behind the other side of the gator that Yasha's fighting, yes. Then for my bonus action, I'm going to cast Healing Word on the little bird creature.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Roll to heal on that one. It's within range.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's six.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Six points of healing. You watch as suddenly, its yellow eye flickers and looks around for a second and it begins to push against the slickness.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: My invoked Jester turns around and thumbs ups to it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys have been plugging everything into this one croc since the beginning of the battle.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I will try to attack. Divine fury, and if I do reckless, does that mean--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you reckless, it'll become regular attacks.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'll do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Make your two strikes. First one with divine fury as you swing down towards the top of its jaws with your blade.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That's a natural one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you go to swing, the jaws tense against you and (groans) it hurts and all your muscles tense from the pain, but you stay focused going for your second strike.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Sorry, hold on. Oh wait, no, I'm doing this one. 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 17 does hit, roll damage. This still gets divine fury though, because it's the first strike that you hit with.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's the first strike, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Man, this is exciting.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so eight--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's not enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (Infernal voice) Crocodile Dundee.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 18 points of damage. How do you want to do this? (cheering)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That heals you up there, you come to consciousness, Nott.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You got the kill? You were barely in the fight!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, he totally came! He's good, he's a hero! Can I use my invoked Jester, my duplicate, to run over to Nott? She can move 30 feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She can, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It wouldn't be encumbered because--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's an illusion. Correct. Here, in this instance, the illusion would not, I don't think, be affected by the rough terrain.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wonderful, I'm going to cast Cure Wounds on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: At, you know--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The lowest level possible.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: At second level. We're about to get a rest, it seems stupid to-- oh, it's cocked. Five plus four. No. It would be plus four, plus four? No, it's 2d8 plus four.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct, at 2nd-level.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay yeah, nine.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Don't tempt me. You guys all take a moment, tend to your exhaustion from plunging into this swamp haphazardly, some of you taking quite a beating in the process. Jester guides your attention to this now struggling small bird creature. It looks to be about the size of a an adolescent child, maybe no more than-- well, let's say a little younger than that, maybe like an eight or nine year old. But instead of skin, it's just black feathers. No wings really, the feathers are too small among the arm to actually be flight-ready. The lower half of its body is still sunken and thick in the bog. It has, where its face is, two bright yellow eyes, very crow, raven-like eyes and a long black pointed beak. And (scared bird croaking).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And welcome back everybody. Before we get back into the game, we have the winner of our fantastic Wyrmwood giveaway from the break: akpresto. Akpresto, congratulations, you won the awesome, awesome-wood-full game dice tray from Wyrmwood. We'll get that to you ASAP, thank you for coming in and everybody else who jumped into the chat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It smells a little like tuna, and that's not me, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's disgusting.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ew!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You can't burp and then try to blame the smell on somebody else.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm telling you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's really foul.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Out of context quotes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Guys!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, sorry Matt, is there something you'd like to discuss with the table?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A purple worm erupts from the middle of the floor--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You've heard of various different strange races out there. Bird people may have crossed in a story before but-- You've done a little bit of study. Make a nature check for you, Caleb, since you studied in this area, just general knowledge.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Stuck here for a while. Not their fault. Don't want them to die for my carelessness." Closes the book (chirps).

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (sadly) We've got to get him to his family.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do you know where they are heading?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (chirps)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I don't know. Are you a--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A boy bird, or a girl bird, or just a bird bird?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: A word bird.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Girl."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Girl bird! Kiri! Oh no. How many sisters do you have?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Puts up four fingers with long, exaggerated feathered fingertips.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Would I recognize what species or race of people?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You haven't encountered these people before.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Empire kids wouldn't know?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Empire kids wouldn't know? The whole studied thing?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Looking through it and making out the layout of it, it was definitely a post house for Berleben when it was first made. The stones look well over 100 years old. They have long been reclaimed by the roots and vines of the swamp. Nothing special beyond that really. Just an abandoned post.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Holding the dagger, she goes forward and starts pecking out of your hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay. Ow. Ow!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have some pastries, too, if you like sweets.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hey Caleb, you got a second?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The head turns to you as well. Takes it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Many seconds. What do you need?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Take a look at this. Do these mean anything to you? Just looking at the stone structures that are here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You rolled investigation?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I rolled an eight. They are stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It looks like it's very old and has long fallen over and has been absorbed mostly by the swamp. It doesn't look like it was a large structure. Like it might have been a small, brief gatehouse off the path. Some sort of a watch post that had--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Since he brought me over, what do I make of the same?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. That is a 25.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "A round for everyone? You too?" Points to the little hooded creature next to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That was a shitty roll. Eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's too dark in here, unfortunately. There's only six other patrons, but they're too dimly lit for you to make out any features.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I get up close to them and one by one?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We can look in a few more minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let your eyes adjust to the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As Jester begins walking off through the chamber, he's like, "What can I get you guys? What you asking for? What you looking for?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What's your strongest drink?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That would be the Labenda Throat Grog."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What is-- what?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Labenda Throat Grog."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. That.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Burns you like a fucker."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Bender Throat Grog?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Labenda. The swamp you're in. Same color as the swamp. Burns your throat. Tastes like grog. That's where it got its name."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do you mean, same color as the swamp? Green and shitty?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That sounds exciting. I think we need a round.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "No, I wanted some entertainment to liven the place up a bit. I've been here for ten years, and it's been a bit dull."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We heard amazing things about this area, and we thought we would check it out, because we're travelers.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Historical point of interest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, and we like to see different sorts of things, and this is really an interesting area. You know, not many cities have a lot of water in them like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's a shithole. But it's our shithole!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's really cool-looking, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you, I appreciate that."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's exactly the magnet that I bought when I got in here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, you got one of the shithole magnets, too?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I got a shithole magnet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If anyone else wants any, I got a whole bucket of them."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You are a shithole magnet, really, when you think about it. That's fair.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It spoke to me.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're building a stage for something? Is there some sort of circus in town?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's been here since the empire started. Almost three centuries."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's a shithole. But it's our shithole!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's really cool-looking, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you, I appreciate that."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's exactly the magnet that I bought when I got in here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, you got one of the shithole magnets, too?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I got a shithole magnet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If anyone else wants any, I got a whole bucket of them."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You are a shithole magnet, really, when you think about it. That's fair.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It spoke to me.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're building a stage for something? Is there some sort of circus in town?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, I wanted some entertainment to liven the place up a bit. I've been here for ten years, and it's been a bit dull."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did you own the previous bar that this one's built on top of?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I did. Inherited it from my father, before it, you know."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How old is Berleben in general?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "That's not a bad idea." He makes a note on a piece of paper. "There will be, in a few weeks."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: There's a fancier part of this town?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's The Stilts. It's where it's all raised up. That's where the Summit House is, where the Starosta stays. There's the Mossmire Temple, which is where the druid that keeps the swamp fane stays. The Idolmaster."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're also going to be looking for a place to sleep. I don't know if you'd recommend anywhere that--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You're already here."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You have rooms?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah!" He points down.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's exciting!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Downstairs?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whoa, that could be so much fun!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is it haunted?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Do you like things that are haunted?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's haunted!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is it submerged in water?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, we cleared out underneath. Everything that fell in, we cleared out, and we have subterranean rooms."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm very excited by this.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do they have portholes to the water?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Look. It was easy work, mate. All right? Meet his friends, lead them to the safe house in the swamp, lead them back. Good pay. Simple work. And yeah, I'm going to miss it, huh? But I value my life more than that. Few weeks back I escorted one of his smuggled troops to the safe house in the swamp. Right? The one you're asking about. Left them there with instruction to return a few days later and escort them to the underground river. Returned and I heard something cruel. Some low, echoing howl. (weak scream) It was much scarier than that. Shaking, I got there, right? Fucking hell. Outside the house, all of them, dead. Strung up in reeds and roots. Bloated and left as a warning, that's what I say. I hear the howl again, and I just run."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an intimidation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Beau gives a crack.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're so intimidating, Caleb. Really, really--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is a 15.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A 15? The sweat begins to build a little more on his brow. He's like, "Well, I was just-- don't need to be taking me back to him. You know, I don't want the work anymore."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, you know  keep it simple, stupid.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (sighs) "Could I get another drink? Look mate, it's some shit going on out there in the swamp. I bet he didn't tell you anything, did he?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Was it gators?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, it wasn't gators!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh well, because we are really good at killing those.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We are very aware that he has sent us into something more troublesome than he has described, and we are willing to do that. We're told you are the one who can get us in the right direction? We'll be very disappointed otherwise.
